title: Workbook — The Incarnation
html:
Student Workbook

For use after class or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Remembering the Essentials

  1. What does the phrase «The Word became flesh» (Jn 1:14) mean?
  2. What are the four reasons the Word became flesh, according to the Catechism?
  3. What did Mary answer the angel Gabriel at the Annunciation?
  4. What does it mean that Jesus is true God and true man?
  5. What does the title Theotokos that the Church gives to Mary mean?

For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no «correct» answers to these personal questions.

1. What does it mean to you that God chose to become a human body, with all its limitations?

Think about how vulnerable a baby is: totally dependent on others to survive. God chose to start that way. What does that tell you about how God relates to us?

 

 

2. Mary said «yes» without knowing everything that would come after. Is there a «yes» God is asking of you today that you are afraid to give?

It could be a big or small decision. Write about that situation and, next to it, what you would need to be able to say «let it be done» like Mary.

 

 

3. If Jesus is truly man and experienced weariness, hunger, sadness, and temptation, how does that change how you see your own sufferings?

Does it help you feel understood? Give you hope? Or is it hard to believe that God really understands what it means to be human?

 

 

4. «God became man so that man might become God.» What do you think this phrase means for your daily life?

How would your day-to-day life change if you truly lived convinced that you are called to share in God’s life?

 

 

5. Mary is a model of faith. Which quality of Mary would you like to imitate in your relationship with God?

Her availability? Her courage? Her silence? Her trust? Write why you would like to have that quality.

 

 

Practical Activities

A. Meditate on Mary’s «Let it be done»

Sit in a quiet place. Imagine the scene of the Annunciation: the room, the light, the angel, Mary. Listen to the angel’s words. Now imagine those words are also for you: «Do not be afraid... the Lord is with you.» What is God saying to you today? Write a brief letter of response, beginning with «Let it be done to me according to your word.»

 

 

 

 

B. Read Philippians 2:6-11 — The hymn of the Incarnation

Paul wrote one of the most beautiful texts in the New Testament about Christ’s self-emptying. Read it aloud. Underline the words that describe what Christ gave up (his divine condition) and those that describe what he took on (the condition of a servant). Write a three-line reflection on what strikes you most in this text.

 

 

 

C. Look up an image of the Annunciation

Search online for Fra Angelico’s «Annunciation» or Leonardo da Vinci’s. Observe it in silence for 5 minutes. Notice the details: the colors, the postures, the expressions, the light. What does this image tell you about the mystery of the Incarnation? Write your impressions.

 

 

 

 

Memory Verse

«And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we have seen his glory, glory as of the only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth»

— John 1:14

Prayer for This Week

Lord Jesus Christ, Son of God made man,
thank you for choosing to share our flesh.
You were not ashamed to become like us,
to weep our tears, to bear our wounds.
Teach me to receive you as Mary received you:
with an open heart, without reserve, without fear.
May your will be done in me,
today and always.
Amen.

Print Workbook
